title: Projects
text:
Keywords: volatility forecasting, options trading, GARCH, machine learning, LSTM, QuantConnect
This project develops a systematic volatility forecasting and options trading framework for SPY short-dated options. We modeled annualized 5-day realized volatility using GARCH-family models, Random Forests, feedforward neural networks, and LSTM-based models, and compared predicted realized volatility with at-the-money implied volatility to construct volatility-edge trading signals.
The strategy was implemented and backtested in QuantConnect using long/short straddles and strangles with explicit entry, exit, and risk-management rules.
Read Paper
Methods: GARCH, EGARCH, GJR-GARCH, Random Forest, feedforward neural networks, LSTM, volatility forecasting, options backtesting
Tools: Python, QuantConnect, scikit-learn, arch, pandas, TensorFlow/PyTorch

title: Projects
section: Chess Anomaly Detection with Metropolis-Hastings Sampling
text:
Chess Anomaly Detection with Metropolis-Hastings Sampling
Keywords: anomaly detection, Metropolis-Hastings, Bayesian computation, chess analytics, FastAPI
This project develops a probabilistic framework for detecting unusually strong chess play by comparing observed move sequences against a counterfactual human baseline. The method uses Maia-based human move proposals and Stockfish-based centipawn-loss scoring within a Metropolis-Hastings sampling framework.
We also built a FastAPI service to make the sampler reproducible and easier to run.
Methods: Metropolis-Hastings sampling, empirical p-value testing, Markov chain diagnostics, anomaly detection
Tools: Python, FastAPI, Docker, Stockfish, Polars

title: Projects
section: Web-Scraped Recipe Dataset Construction and Interactive Analysis
text:
Web-Scraped Recipe Dataset Construction and Interactive Analysis
Keywords: web scraping, data cleaning, R Shiny, interactive analysis, data validation
This project builds an automated web-scraping pipeline to collect and structure recipe data from the official game Wiki for Stardew Valley. The pipeline extracts recipe structure, ingredient composition, and numerical buff effects from heterogeneous HTML pages, with parsing and validation workflows designed to improve data quality and reproducibility.
We also developed core analysis logic to support recipe search, craftable recipe identification, and minimal-additional-ingredient recommendations. The final output includes an interactive R Shiny application that enables flexible exploration of the dataset and supports high-usability recipe analysis workflows.
GitHub Repository
Methods: web scraping, HTML parsing, data validation, rule-based filtering, interactive application development
Tools: R, R Shiny, rvest, dplyr, stringr

title: Projects
text:
Insurance Subrogation Outcome Prediction
Keywords: supervised learning, classification, insurance analytics, model evaluation
This project focuses on predicting insurance subrogation outcomes using claim-level information, loss severity indicators, and recovery-related attributes. I performed end-to-end preprocessing and feature engineering, including categorical encoding, interaction terms, and distributional transformations.
I developed and compared multiple tree-based models, including XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost, and TabM. The final modeling pipeline emphasized reproducible experimentation, cross-validation, and out-of-sample robustness.
Methods: classification, feature engineering, cross-validation, model comparison, ensemble learning
Tools: Python, XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost, scikit-learn, SHAP

title: Home
text:
Simeng Wu

M.S. Student in Statistical Science, Duke University

Statistical Modeling · Missing Data · Bayesian Methods · Applied Machine Learning


I am a Master’s student in Statistical Science at Duke University. Before Duke, I studied Statistics, Economics, and Finance at University College London.
My interests span statistical modeling, missing data, Bayesian methods, machine learning, and data-driven decision making. I enjoy projects that combine rigorous statistical thinking with practical implementation.
